title: Spicy Gooey Peruvian Potato Gratin
description: A creamy, spicy potato gratin inspired by Peruvian flavors, using charlotte potatoes and vegan cheese for a gooey, eggfree finish perfect for meal prep.
instructions: While the oven heats, slice the charlotte potatoes into 6 mm thick rounds.
In a skillet, heat the olive oil over medium heat and sauté the onion, garlic, and ginger until fragrant and translucent, about 3–4 minutes.
Add the smoked paprika, cumin, and cayenne pepper to the skillet and stir for 30 seconds until aromatic.
Pour in the coconut milk, bring the mixture to a gentle simmer, and season with salt and pepper; let it thicken slightly for 2 minutes.

Arrange a layer of potato slices in a greased baking dish, then pour over a portion of the coconut‑milk sauce. Sprinkle a layer of vegan shredded cheese. Repeat the layers until all ingredients are used, finishing with a cheese layer.
Cover the dish with aluminum foil and bake for 40 minutes at 190 °C. Remove the foil and bake an additional 10 minutes, or until the top is golden and bubbly.
Let the gratin rest for 5 minutes before serving. Garnish with fresh cilantro if desired.
